headline: SAMSUNG (005930.KS) — Q2 preliminary Op profit KRW 89.4T (~$58.4B), a 19x YoY surge and above the KRW 85.5T sell-side consensus; revenue KRW 171T; shares -6-7% intraday Tuesday in Seoul on capex/demand-peak worries and on read that HBM leadership still sits with SK Hynix inside the Nvidia supply chain; brokerage debate shifts from 'can they beat' to 'is memory pricing already at cycle peak' as DRAM ASP +44% and NAND ASP +53% QoQ
summary: Samsung Electronics released its Q2 2026 preliminary earnings pre-open Tuesday July 7 (Seoul session): operating profit KRW 89.4 trillion (~$58.4B) vs. KRW 57.2T prior quarter and the FnGuide consensus KRW 85.5T — a 19x YoY increase driven by DRAM/NAND/HBM pricing and volume, with average DRAM prices +44% QoQ and NAND +53% QoQ. Revenue KRW 171T vs. KRW 172.2T consensus. Despite the beat on OP, Samsung shares closed nearly 7% lower in Seoul (had traded down more than 6% at the open) as investors flagged (a) capex commitment escalation implied by the print, (b) the risk memory prices are now at cycle peak, and (c) ongoing SK Hynix HBM leadership within Nvidia's supply chain leaving Samsung the lower-margin qualifier. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE-TO-NEUTRAL for MU into fiscal-Q4 print — the '~7% sell despite record OP' template is a template for how MU could trade even on a beat; (2) NEUTRAL-TO-POSITIVE for 000660.KS (SK Hynix) heading into its July 10 SKHY Nasdaq ADR debut at $166 (Alibaba-topping $29.65B raise) — the Samsung capex worry validates the SK Hynix HBM-first framing; (3) POSITIVE for ASML/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC — Samsung's capex escalation is the fab-equipment demand signal, and Bernstein raising ASML PT to $2,623 (from $1,971) hours later fits the same read; (4) neutral for NVDA/AMD short-cycle.

headline: SPACEX (SPCX) — Nasdaq-100 inclusion completes pre-open Tuesday July 7 at ~1% index weight; JPMorgan estimates $4.3B QQQ forced buy alone with total passive demand across Nasdaq-100 / Russell trackers up to ~$27B into a 3-5% float; SPCX closed near $162 last week vs. $150 IPO and $200+ post-listing high; Swissquote's Ozkardeskaya warns the add 'increases volatility and challenges [Nasdaq-100's] ability to represent fundamentals' given Musk's 80%+ voting control and 100x-plus sales multiple
summary: SpaceX (SPCX) officially entered the Nasdaq-100 before market open Tuesday July 7, 2026 under Nasdaq's new fast-track rule (top-40 market cap post-IPO = 15-trading-day eligibility, effective May 1, 2026), the fastest major-index inclusion after IPO in modern market history. JPMorgan forecasts ~$4.3B of forced buying from QQQ alone, with total passive demand across Nasdaq-100 and adjacent Russell trackers up to ~$27B — into a float of only 3-5% of shares outstanding (Musk plus pre-IPO private holders retain the remainder). SPCX closed near $162 last week vs. its $150 IPO opening price and a post-listing high above $200. Expected index weight ~1%. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MECHANICAL positive for Nasdaq-100 headline level (net add, no removal); (2) MIX-SHIFT drag on every incumbent QQQ constituent as passive weights re-cut — heaviest bleed for MSFT/AAPL/NVDA/GOOGL/META and by extension for the House-covered mega-caps; (3) forced-buy imbalance could compress secondary market liquidity for the highest-beta names into and out of the open; (4) Swissquote's Ipek Ozkardeskaya cautioned the add 'increases volatility and challenges [Nasdaq-100's] ability to represent fundamentals' given Musk's 80%+ voting control and 100x-plus sales valuation — a structural framing likely to appear in end-of-week sell-side notes.

headline: AMD — Goldman Sachs raises PT to $640 from $450 (+42%) Monday July 6 (Buy reiterated); Toshya Hari anchors the call in agentic-AI CPU demand ('not a short-lived phenomenon'); AMD +10.5% intraday Monday on the note, joining Cantor's $700 top-of-Street PT (Jun 29), UBS $670 (Jun 29), Wells Fargo $615 (Jul 1); Goldman also lifted Teradyne (TER) PT to $465 from $350 (+33%) Buy on ATE demand tailwind
summary: Goldman Sachs analyst Toshya Hari raised AMD's price target to $640 from $450 Monday July 6, 2026, reiterating a Buy rating and anchoring the reset in a bullish agentic-AI CPU demand thesis — Hari wrote 'we believe the demand for CPUs required by agentic AI will not be a short-lived phenomenon.' AMD +10.5% intraday Monday on the note. The reset sits within the broader ex-Cantor sell-side capitulation trade: Cantor Fitzgerald $700 top-of-Street PT (Jun 29), UBS $670 (Jun 29), Wells Fargo $615 (Jul 1). Goldman in the same note lifted Teradyne (TER, not House coverage) PT to $465 from $350, Buy — TER +8%+ intraday. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for AMD as the sell-side model reset accelerates alongside the framing that ex-NVDA hyperscale CPU spend is now material; (2) POSITIVE by extension for INTC (Xeon franchise participates in the same agentic-AI CPU TAM though structurally weaker on IP); (3) POSITIVE for ATE / test complex (TER analog) and for the semi-cap-eq cohort (AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) that supplies the leading-edge fab capacity absorbing the demand.

headline: ASML — Bernstein raises PT to $2,623 (from $1,971) / €2,300 (from €1,700), Outperform reiterated; frames the reset as 'unprecedented AI-driven expansion in both cutting-edge logic and DRAM'; Bernstein now models €80B ASML revenue by 2030 (20% CAGR, 24% above €64B consensus) with EUV alone growing at 30% CAGR to €42.7B; ASML +3.9% Monday intraday
summary: Bernstein raised its ASML price target to $2,623 from $1,971 (~33% increase) on Monday July 6, 2026, reiterating an Outperform rating. The euro-denominated PT lifts to €2,300 from €1,700 (~35% increase). Bernstein now models ASML total revenue reaching €80B by 2030 — a 20% compound annual growth rate through the period and 24% above the current sell-side consensus of €64B. EUV equipment revenue alone is modeled to grow at a 30% CAGR to €42.7B by 2030. Framing is 'unprecedented AI-driven expansion across both cutting-edge logic and DRAM capacity.' ASML +3.9% Monday intraday. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for ASML as the biggest single sell-side PT reset in the semi-cap-eq complex, catching up to Cantor's June AMAT $850 / KLAC $325 / LRCX $500 template; (2) POSITIVE for AMAT / LRCX / KLAC by category re-rating — Morgan Stanley lifted LRCX PT to $404 (from $331) Overweight on the same day, confirming the DRAM-capex tailwind; (3) POSITIVE for 8035.T (Tokyo Electron) and ENTG by fab-equipment demand extension; (4) Samsung's Q2 preliminary print Tuesday (KRW 89.4T OP) is the demand-side confirmation for the same DRAM/HBM capex reset Bernstein just modeled.

headline: LAM RESEARCH (LRCX) — Morgan Stanley raises PT to $404 from $331 (+22%), Overweight reiterated Monday July 6; part of a semi-cap-eq re-rate cascade with Bernstein $2,623 ASML (same day) and Cantor's late-June AMAT $850 / KLAC $325 / LRCX $500 pricing; the group has been the cleanest expression of the memory-capex thesis into Samsung's Tuesday Q2 print
summary: Morgan Stanley raised its price target on Lam Research (LRCX) to $404 from $331 Monday July 6, 2026, maintaining an Overweight rating. The +22% PT reset sits within the same-day semi-cap-eq re-rate cascade that also included Bernstein's ASML PT to $2,623 (from $1,971) and follows Cantor Fitzgerald's late-June wave (AMAT $850, KLAC $325, LRCX $500). The cluster is the cleanest sell-side expression of the DRAM/HBM capex thesis that Samsung's Q2 preliminary confirmed on the demand side (KRW 89.4T operating profit, memory ASPs +44% (DRAM) / +53% (NAND) QoQ). Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE for LRCX at the direct target; (2) POSITIVE for AMAT / KLAC by cluster re-rate as the whole semi-cap-eq complex marks to Bernstein's ASML template; (3) POSITIVE for 8035.T (Tokyo Electron), ENTG and AEHR by category extension; (4) monitor Cantor / Susquehanna / Citi for the next PT resets.

headline: SK HYNIX (000660.KS) — Nasdaq ADR debut confirmed for Friday July 10 under ticker SKHY at ~$166/ADR (10 ADRs = 1 KOSPI share); $29.65B raise (17.79M new shares @ KRW 45.45T) becomes largest ADR listing in market history, surpassing Alibaba's 2014 $21.8B NYSE debut; SK Hynix holds ~60% of the HBM market share into Nvidia's supply chain; pricing lands Thursday July 9
summary: SK Hynix officially confirmed Monday July 6, 2026 that it will list ADRs on the Nasdaq Friday July 10 under the ticker SKHY, at a reference price of approximately $166 per ADR (structure: 10 ADRs = 1 common Korean share). Total capital raise: KRW 45.45 trillion (~$29.65B) via 17.79 million new shares, which would surpass Alibaba's 2014 $21.8B NYSE listing as the largest single ADR IPO in market history. Pricing is Thursday July 9, first trade Friday July 10. SK Hynix holds ~60% of the HBM market share and is the primary HBM supplier inside Nvidia's Blackwell / Rubin GPU supply chain, giving it a materially higher valuation premium than Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) on the same memory macro. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for the 000660.KS position — the ADR pricing at $166 sets a new US-benchmark valuation floor that Korea-listed shares will mark to; (2) POSITIVE for MU as the 'other HBM name' in the trade — SK Hynix ADR liquidity re-rates the HBM peer group; (3) POSITIVE for ASML / AMAT / LRCX / KLAC as fab-equipment beneficiaries of the raised capital being deployed into HBM3E/HBM4 capacity; (4) NEUTRAL for Samsung (005930.KS) — the 6-7% Tuesday sell in Seoul suggests the market is already discounting the SK Hynix HBM-first differential.

headline: TUESDAY OPEN — Nasdaq-100 futures +1.1% (+324.25) into open, S&P 500 futures +0.44% (+33.5), Dow futures -0.04% (-23) as tech-led risk-on continues from Monday's chip-cohort snapback; SMH ETF adds to Monday's +2.7% at the open; SPCX index-add mechanical bid is the dominant flow ahead of the bell
summary: Tuesday July 7, 2026 US pre-open — futures continue the risk-on rotation into tech from Monday's chip-cohort snapback. Nasdaq-100 E-minis +324.25 pts (+1.1%), S&P 500 E-minis +33.5 pts (+0.44%), Dow E-minis -23 pts (-0.04%) at 7:24 a.m. ET. The Dow's slight red vs. Nasdaq's +1.1% is a further extension of Monday's rotation back into semis/hyperscalers and out of Thursday's defensive-Dow leaders. SMH VanEck Semiconductor ETF adds to Monday's +2.7% at the opening bell. Dominant flow into the bell is the SPCX Nasdaq-100 inclusion forced buy (~$4.3B QQQ alone, up to $27B total across trackers) meeting a 3-5% float — outsize opening-auction moves possible in SPCX and index-heavy names (MSFT, AAPL, NVDA, GOOGL, META). Backdrop: Goldman AMD $640 upgrade and Bernstein ASML $2,623 upgrade Monday, Morgan Stanley LRCX $404 same-day, Samsung Q2 preliminary print (KRW 89.4T OP) landed Tuesday Seoul morning. Read-through to House coverage: risk-on tone remains supportive for the semi / DC-hardware / hyperscaler cohort, but the Samsung -7% intraday shows even record beats can trigger 'peak-cycle' selling — key tell for MU / ORCL / TSM into the rest of the week's earnings calendar.

headline: SECTION 232 SEMICONDUCTOR REPORT — Commerce Sec. Lutnick's July 1 report to Trump on the datacenter-semi supply chain still not publicly released as of Tuesday July 7 pre-open, six days past the statutory deadline; the silence extends a binary overhang for NVDA/AMD/INTC/MU/TSM as the report gives Trump statutory authority to raise/expand the current 25% tariff, add licensing conditions, or carve out CHIPS-domestic exemptions; parallel OpenAI 5% US-government-equity-stake proposal remains in early-stage conceptual discussions
summary: Two policy overhangs from last week continue into the Tuesday July 7 open without resolution. (1) SECTION 232 SEMICONDUCTOR-DATACENTER MARKET REPORT — Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick was required under President Trump's January 14 Proclamation 11002 to deliver Trump an updated market study on the US datacenter semiconductor supply chain by July 1, 2026. Six days past deadline (Monday July 6 + Tuesday July 7 pre-open), no public release, no leaked highlights, no press briefing. The report gives Trump statutory authority to (a) leave the current 25% Section 232 tariff on NVDA H200-class / AMD MI325X-class chips untouched, (b) raise or expand it to cover more product codes, (c) pair it with licensing conditions on China-destined shipments, or (d) create carveouts for domestic supply-chain buildout. Lutnick publicly stated South Korean and Taiwanese chipmakers not investing in US capacity could face tariffs 'up to 100%' unless they raise US production. (2) OPENAI 5% US-GOVERNMENT-STAKE — FT and Bloomberg confirmed early-stage discussions between Sam Altman and Trump/Lutnick/Bessent to give the US government a 5% equity stake in each leading US AI lab (Anthropic, GOOGL, META, NVDA, MU, AMD reportedly named), modeled on the Alaska Permanent Fund and requiring congressional action. Read-through: MATERIAL BINARY OVERHANG persists for NVDA/AMD/INTC/MU into any moment this week — the Section 232 delay is longer than the market has typically priced, raising the odds of either (a) a maximalist Trump announcement or (b) private continuation of the current 25% rate; watch for White House statement, Commerce fact-sheet drop, or selective leak.

headline: SIEMENS ENERGY (ENR.DE) — Grid Technologies segment carries the fiscal-2026 guide raise (revenue growth reset to 14-16% from prior 12-14%, book-to-bill 1.72x, all-time-high orders €17.7B, backlog €154B); Schneider Electric in parallel disclosed SF6-free switchgear rollout with Southern California Edison Monday, extending the AI-datacenter grid-hardware demand narrative into the utility procurement layer
summary: Two Europe-listed grid-hardware datapoints reinforce the AI-datacenter power buildout thesis heading into the Tuesday July 7 US open. (1) Siemens Energy (ENR.DE) — the Grid Technologies segment was cited as the primary driver for the fiscal-2026 comparable revenue growth guide raise to 14-16% (from 12-14%), with quarterly all-time-high orders of €17.7B, book-to-bill 1.72x, and total order backlog €154B. Grid Technologies alone modeled at 25-27% comparable revenue growth for FY26. (2) Schneider Electric — announced SF6-free switchgear rollout with Southern California Edison, one of the largest US utilities, expanding the grid-modernization order pipeline into the utility procurement layer that historically has been slower to move. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for GEV (Grid Solutions US analog to Siemens Grid Tech) — the Siemens book-to-bill 1.72x is a direct read on GEV's addressable market; (2) POSITIVE for ETN (Boyd Thermal + core power distribution) — the Schneider SF6-free California win extends the utility-grade demand curve; (3) POSITIVE for VRT — the Siemens guide raise validates the '$726B AI-datacenter capex' Dell'Oro framework; (4) POSITIVE for ABB (not currently in House coverage) as the other pure grid-hardware peer.

headline: OKLO (OKLO) — Groves criticality still pending; DOE readiness review remains the next binary as Tuesday July 7 opens with no new milestone since the July 1 Documented Safety Analysis approval; company continues to guide 'July 2026' for first self-sustaining chain reaction with fuel load / startup testing ahead; Meta 6.6-GW Vistra + TerraPower + Oklo nuclear PPA framework remains the largest customer-facing catalyst supporting the multi-GW commercial thesis
summary: Tracking item Tuesday July 7 pre-open — Oklo's Atomic Alchemy subsidiary has not confirmed first criticality of the Groves Isotope Test Reactor. The most recent milestone remains the July 1 DOE approval of the Documented Safety Analysis (DSA), removing the last major regulatory hurdle before startup. Remaining path: (1) DOE readiness review; (2) DOE startup approval; (3) fuel load; (4) startup testing; (5) first self-sustaining chain reaction. Company guidance still 'July 2026.' Broader context: Meta's January 2026 6.6-GW nuclear PPA framework covering TerraPower Natrium SFR + Vistra + Oklo Aurora SFR + Constellation restart remains the largest customer-facing catalyst for the SMR/microreactor complex. Amazon Talen expanded to 1,920 MW and Microsoft-Constellation 837 MW TMI restart round out the commercial framework. Read-through: (1) NEUTRAL for OKLO — no news is neither positive nor negative until the readiness-review clock actually starts; (2) NEUTRAL-POSITIVE for SMR / X-energy (XE) / CEG as the complex trades as a group and the underlying customer PPA book continues to expand.
